rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because reviewing this technical passage requires assessing whether the optimization claims and conclusions follow from the stated premises.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the section makes empirical and theoretical claims that should be checked against the reported support within the artifact.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central because the artifact explicitly claims a first convergence rate and positions itself against prior aggregation work.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central because reviewing a section includes judging whether the paragraph order and narrative arc support the argument.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central because a generic section review normally includes sentence-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship is central because the section invokes named prior works and uses them to support methodological and rate claims.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active because the reviewer must distinguish what can be inferred from the section alone from what would require the full paper or cited works.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active because the section contains broad claims about first results, optimal rates, and equivalence to synthesis guarantees.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active because the delegation asks only for review of this section, not rewriting or executing changes.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because unchecked technical or novelty claims in a paper section could mislead later readers or reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active because any review responsibility should be grounded in the supplied artifact rather than unsupported external assumptions.
